approach_summary: Read the intellij-code-reference SKILL.md to confirm strategy. Used mcp__idea__search_symbol to locate the class (single hit) and confirm it's a Spring @Component('OrderCommonRuleService') implementing SimpleRuleService<Map<String,Object>>. Used mcp__idea__search_in_files_by_text on the class name to enumerate every reference project-wide — only one non-declaration hit: OrderRuleService extending it. Since no direct method call sites exist, followed the framework-dispatch pattern the skill recommends: searched for SimpleRuleService usages and found DefaultOrganizationAuditRuleService.audit() at line 111 calling ruleEngineService.getSimpleRuleService(rule.getRules()).execute(parameters) — this is the real (polymorphic) caller, with bean name coming from the DB-stored AuditRule.rules column. Confirmed the literal string 'OrderCommonRuleService' appears nowhere except the @Component declaration, so there are no hardcoded bean-name references. Ran a regex cross-check for any 'OrderCommonRuleService.' or 'OrderCommonRuleService var' patterns to be sure nothing is called on a typed reference. Reported self-call of supports() inside execute(), the inheritance relationship with OrderRuleService (override of execute, inherited supports, no super-calls), and the single framework dispatch site.
